questions.q: Paragraph:An ellipse whose major axis is parallel to $x$-axis such that segment of a focal chord are 1 and 2 units. The line $\frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}+\frac{1}{c}=0$ are the chords of the ellipse such that a, c, b are in H.P. and are bisected by the point at which they are concurrent, the equation of auxiliary circle is $2 x^2+2 y^2+4 p x+4 q y-16 p+1=0$ thenQuestion:The centre of ellipse is
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{c}, \mathrm{b}$ are in H.P.
$\Rightarrow \frac{2}{c}=\frac{1}{a}+\frac{1}{b}$
Line $\frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}+\frac{1}{c}=0$ passes through a fixed point $\left(-\frac{1}{2},-\frac{1}{2}\right)$
So centre of ellipse is $\left(-\frac{1}{2},-\frac{1}{2}\right)$
Which is also centre of auxiliary circle
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{p}=\frac{1}{2}, \mathrm{q}=\frac{1}{2}$ radius of auxiliary circle $=2$
$\therefore$ Length of major axis $=4$ also it segment of focal chord are 1,2
$\Rightarrow \frac{1}{1}+\frac{1}{2}=\frac{4}{b^2} \Rightarrow b^2=\frac{8}{3}$
Director circle $\left(x+\frac{1}{2}\right)^2+\left(y+\frac{1}{2}\right)^2=\frac{8}{3}+4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: If $a^2+\mathrm{by}^2+2 \mathrm{hxy}+2 \mathrm{gx}+2 \mathrm{fy}+\mathrm{c}=0$ represents an ellipse, then $\mathrm{h}^2 \lt \mathrm{ab}$ and $\mathrm{abc}+2 \mathrm{fgh}-\mathrm{af}^2-\mathrm{bg}^2-\mathrm{ch}^2 \neq 0$. If for every point ( $\mathrm{x}_1, \mathrm{y}_1$ ) satisfying above equation $\left(2 h-x_1, 2 k-y_1\right)$ also satisfy it, then $(h, k)$ is centre of it. The length of semi major axis and minor axis is nothing but the maximum and minimum value of the distance of a point lying on the curve from its centre.Question: If the given ellipse is rotated so that it has its major axis coincident with x-axis, then its equation becomes

questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: If $a^2+\mathrm{by}^2+2 \mathrm{hxy}+2 \mathrm{gx}+2 \mathrm{fy}+\mathrm{c}=0$ represents an ellipse, then $\mathrm{h}^2 \lt \mathrm{ab}$ and $\mathrm{abc}+2 \mathrm{fgh}-\mathrm{af}^2-\mathrm{bg}^2-\mathrm{ch}^2 \neq 0$. If for every point ( $\mathrm{x}_1, \mathrm{y}_1$ ) satisfying above equation $\left(2 h-x_1, 2 k-y_1\right)$ also satisfy it, then $(h, k)$ is centre of it. The length of semi major axis and minor axis is nothing but the maximum and minimum value of the distance of a point lying on the curve from its centre.Question: The maximum area of the circle lying inside the given ellipse is
questions.solution: Centre of circle be origin and its radius is the length of semi minor axis Hence area $=\pi$

questions.q: Paragraph:An ellipse whose major axis is parallel to $x$-axis such that segment of a focal chord are 1 and 2 units. The line $\frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}+\frac{1}{c}=0$ are the chords of the ellipse such that a, c, b are in H.P. and are bisected by the point at which they are concurrent, the equation of auxiliary circle is $2 x^2+2 y^2+4 p x+4 q y-16 p+1=0$ thenQuestion:Equation of director circle is
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{c}, \mathrm{b}$ are in H.P.
$\Rightarrow \frac{2}{c}=\frac{1}{a}+\frac{1}{b}$
Line $\frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}+\frac{1}{c}=0$ passes through a fixed point $\left(-\frac{1}{2},-\frac{1}{2}\right)$
So centre of ellipse is $\left(-\frac{1}{2},-\frac{1}{2}\right)$
Which is also centre of auxiliary circle
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{p}=\frac{1}{2}, \mathrm{q}=\frac{1}{2}$ radius of auxiliary circle $=2$
$\therefore$ Length of major axis $=4$ also it segment of focal chord are 1,2
$\Rightarrow \frac{1}{1}+\frac{1}{2}=\frac{4}{b^2} \Rightarrow b^2=\frac{8}{3}$
Director circle $\left(x+\frac{1}{2}\right)^2+\left(y+\frac{1}{2}\right)^2=\frac{8}{3}+4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: If $a^2+\mathrm{by}^2+2 \mathrm{hxy}+2 \mathrm{gx}+2 \mathrm{fy}+\mathrm{c}=0$ represents an ellipse, then $\mathrm{h}^2 \lt \mathrm{ab}$ and $\mathrm{abc}+2 \mathrm{fgh}-\mathrm{af}^2-\mathrm{bg}^2-\mathrm{ch}^2 \neq 0$. If for every point ( $\mathrm{x}_1, \mathrm{y}_1$ ) satisfying above equation $\left(2 h-x_1, 2 k-y_1\right)$ also satisfy it, then $(h, k)$ is centre of it. The length of semi major axis and minor axis is nothing but the maximum and minimum value of the distance of a point lying on the curve from its centre.Question: For the ellipse $2 x^2-2 x y+4 y^2-(3+\sqrt{2})=0$, the inclination of major axis of it with $x$-axis is
questions.solution:
Taking point $(r \cos \theta, r \sin \theta)$, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
& r^2=\frac{3+\sqrt{2}}{3-\sqrt{2} \sin \left(2 \theta+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)} \\
& r_{\max }=\sqrt{\frac{3+\sqrt{2}}{3-\sqrt{2}}}, r_{\min }=1
\end{aligned}
$$

Max exist when $2 \theta+\frac{\pi}{4}=\frac{\pi}{2} \Rightarrow \theta=\frac{\pi}{8}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: On the ellipse $E=\frac{x^2}{64}+\frac{y^2}{9}=1$, tangents drawn at the point $P_1, P_2, P_3, \ldots \ldots P_n$ on the ellipse intersecting the major axis at $T_1, T_2, T_3 \ldots \ldots, T_n$ respectively.Question: If the value of $\sum_{i=1}^n \frac{\operatorname{Area}\left(\Delta P_i T_i S\right) \cdot \operatorname{Area}\left(\Delta P_i T_i S^{\prime}\right)}{\left(P_i T_i\right)^2}=18$. where $S$ and $S^{\prime}$ represents the foci of the ellipse, then ' $n$ ' equal to:
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { (i) } \quad S A \perp P_i T_i \\ & S^{\prime} B \perp P_i T_i\end{aligned}$
$$ \begin{aligned} & A_1=\operatorname{Area}\left(\Delta S P_i T_i\right)=\frac{1}{2} \times S A \times P_i T_i \\ & A_2=\operatorname{Area}\left(\Delta S^{\prime} P_i T_i\right)=\frac{1}{2} \times S^{\prime} B \times P_i T_i \\ & \frac{A_1 A_2}{\left(P_i T_i\right)^2}=\frac{b^2}{4} \quad\left(S A \times S^{\prime} B=b^2\right) \\ & \sum \frac{A_1 A_2}{\left(P_i T_i\right)^2}=18 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \frac{n b^2}{4}=18 \quad \Rightarrow n=8 \end{aligned} $$ (ii) Tangent at $\left(a e, \frac{b^2}{a}\right)$ $$ \frac{x \cdot a e}{a^2}+\frac{y \cdot b^2}{a \cdot b^2}=1 $$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad e x+y=a \\ & A=\frac{2 a}{e} \cdot \frac{2 a}{2}=\frac{2 a^2}{e}=2 \times 64 \times \frac{8}{\sqrt{55}} \quad \Rightarrow \quad \lambda=64 \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: On the ellipse $E=\frac{x^2}{64}+\frac{y^2}{9}=1$, tangents drawn at the point $P_1, P_2, P_3, \ldots \ldots P_n$ on the ellipse intersecting the major axis at $T_1, T_2, T_3 \ldots \ldots, T_n$ respectively.Question: If the area of the quadrilateral formed by the tangents at the ends of the latus rectum of the ellipse $E$, is $\frac{16 \lambda}{\sqrt{55}}$, then $\lambda$ equals:
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { (i) } \quad S A \perp P_i T_i \\ & S^{\prime} B \perp P_i T_i\end{aligned}$
$$ \begin{aligned} & A_1=\operatorname{Area}\left(\Delta S P_i T_i\right)=\frac{1}{2} \times S A \times P_i T_i \\ & A_2=\operatorname{Area}\left(\Delta S^{\prime} P_i T_i\right)=\frac{1}{2} \times S^{\prime} B \times P_i T_i \\ & \frac{A_1 A_2}{\left(P_i T_i\right)^2}=\frac{b^2}{4} \quad\left(S A \times S^{\prime} B=b^2\right) \\ & \sum \frac{A_1 A_2}{\left(P_i T_i\right)^2}=18 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \frac{n b^2}{4}=18 \quad \Rightarrow n=8 \end{aligned} $$ (ii) Tangent at $\left(a e, \frac{b^2}{a}\right)$ $$ \frac{x \cdot a e}{a^2}+\frac{y \cdot b^2}{a \cdot b^2}=1 $$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad e x+y=a \\ & A=\frac{2 a}{e} \cdot \frac{2 a}{2}=\frac{2 a^2}{e}=2 \times 64 \times \frac{8}{\sqrt{55}} \quad \Rightarrow \quad \lambda=64 \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Tangents are drawn from a variable point $P$ to the ellipse $x^2+4 y^2=4$ meeting it at $Q \& R$. So $Q R$ is chord of contact of pair of tangents drawn from P . On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: If the sum of inclination of PQ and PR is constant $\left(\neq 90^{\circ}\right)$, then the locus of P is a -
questions.solution:
Ellipse $\frac{x^2}{4}+y^2=1$
Let $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{h}, \mathrm{k})$ and equation of any tangent is
$\begin{aligned}
& y=m x \pm \sqrt{4 m^2+1} \Rightarrow(k-m h)^2=4 m^2+1 \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{~m}^2\left(\mathrm{~h}^2-4\right)-2 \mathrm{mkh}+\left(\mathrm{k}^2-1\right)=0 \\
& \theta_1+\theta_2=\alpha \\
& \tan \left(\theta_1+\theta_2\right)=\tan \alpha \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{m}_1+\mathrm{m}_2}{1-\mathrm{m}_1 \mathrm{~m}_2}=\tan \alpha \Rightarrow \frac{\frac{2 \mathrm{hk}}{\mathrm{~h}^2-4}}{1-\frac{\mathrm{k}^2-1}{\mathrm{~h}^2-4}}=\tan \alpha
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \quad 2 \mathrm{xy}=\tan \alpha\left(\mathrm{x}^2-\mathrm{y}^2-3\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{x}^2-2 \mathrm{xy} \cot \alpha-\mathrm{y}^2=3 \\
& \Delta=3-0-0-0+3(\cot \alpha)^2 \neq 0
\end{aligned}$
$\mathrm{H}^2\gt\mathrm{AB}$ so hyperbola
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
